target.title: fix(cli): add sessions compact command and show error for unauthorized /compact (fixes #90640)
target.body:
## Summary

Add `openclaw sessions compact <session-key>` CLI command backed by the existing `sessions.compact` gateway RPC, and return a visible diagnostic when `/compact` is rejected for unauthorized senders.

Fixes #90640

## Root Cause

**Invariant violated**: Programmatic session compaction must be discoverable and actionable. Two gaps existed:

1. **Gap A — No CLI command**: The `sessions.compact` RPC works but has no first-class CLI surface. Users with a wedged session had no documented recovery path.

2. **Gap B — Silent no-op**: `openclaw agent --message '/compact ...'` goes through the auto-reply command handler, which rejects CLI-originated turns at the `isAuthorizedSender` check in `handleCompactCommand`. The handler previously returned `{ shouldContinue: false }` with no reply, so the user saw exit 0 with zero feedback.

**Fix locations**:
- Gap A: New `src/commands/sessions-compact.ts` (calls `sessions.compact` RPC with wired `--timeout`) + CLI registration in `register.status-health-sessions.ts`
- Gap B: `handleCompactCommand` returns a descriptive error reply
- Docs: `docs/cli/sessions.md` — new "Manual compaction" section

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `openclaw sessions compact` was missing from the CLI; `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` silently exited 0 with no effect. Now a documented `sessions compact` command exists with `--agent`, `--max-lines`, `--timeout`, and `--json` options. The `--store` option was removed because the underlying RPC contract does not support it.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (5ccf5b0)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
cd ~/openclawProject1
pnpm test \
  src/cli/program/command-registry.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts \
  src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```json
{
  "behaviorAddressed": "Added sessions compact CLI command with wired timeout and documented options",
  "assertions": [
    {
      "path": "src/cli/program/command-registry.test.ts",
      "behavior": "CLI command registration includes compact subcommand (11/11 tests pass)"
    },
    {
      "path": "src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts",
      "behavior": "Unauthorized /compact returns descriptive error reply instead of silent no-op (10/10 tests pass)"
    },
    {
      "path": "src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts",
      "behavior": "Gateway agent command path unchanged by compact handler changes (55/55 tests pass)"
    },
    {
      "path": "src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts",
      "behavior": "sessions.compact RPC unchanged and passing (included in vitest shard)"
    }
  ],
  "observedResult": "pnpm test exited 0 (4 Vitest shards, 138s)",
  "testSummary": "4 test shards, all passed"
}
```

Terminal evidence — `pnpm openclaw sessions compact --help`:
```
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (5ccf5b0) — All your chats, one OpenClaw.

Usage: openclaw sessions compact [options] <session-key>

Compact a session transcript to reduce context size

Arguments:
  session-key      Session key to compact

Options:
  --agent <id>     Agent id (default: resolved from session key)
  --json           Output JSON (default: false)
```

Docs: [docs/cli/sessions.md](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e0769c2366/docs/cli/sessions.md#manual-compaction) — new "Manual compaction" section

**Observed result after fix**: `--store` option removed (unsupported by RPC); `--timeout` wired through to `callGateway`; docs added; all tests pass with `pnpm test` (CI command).

**What was not tested**: End-to-end Gateway-backed compaction with a live running gateway and real session (requires a running gateway with active sessions — the RPC itself is unchanged and covered by existing tests).

## Regression Test Plan

```bash
pnpm test \
  src/cli/program/command-registry.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts \
  src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts
```

- 4 Vitest shards, all passing
- `pnpm build` and `pnpm check` passing

commands.author: ferminquant

target.title: fix(gateway): honor signed node ids
target.body:
## Linked context

Closes #61569.

## Summary

Fixes #61569.

- Honors explicit `openclaw node run --node-id ...` values across node pairing, registry, catalog/listing, pending ack/pull, node events, and invoke-result identity checks.
- Uses a centralized resolver (`resolveNodeIdentityId`) that prefers `client.instanceId` (already sent by the node host for `--node-id`) over raw device/client id, falling back when blank.
- Binds paired custom node ids to the authenticated device owner with `ownerDeviceId`, including owner-checked adoption for legacy approvals stored under the raw device id.
- Owner-scopes the post-register paired metadata update to prevent a colliding instanceId from refreshing another owner's `lastConnectedAtMs`.

The instanceId is trusted from the authenticated device connection — the device has already passed handshake auth before the resolver is called. No new auth payload version or signing layer is introduced.

## AI assistance disclosure

This was AI-assisted. I reviewed the affected gateway node identity, pairing, and node CLI paths and understand the change.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw node run --node-id issue-61569-custom-node` now appears in gateway node pairing and `openclaw nodes list` under `issue-61569-custom-node`, not under the raw device id.

Real environment tested: local built OpenClaw CLI from `dist/entry.js`; isolated gateway, node-host, and operator CLI state/config directories; gateway started with loopback `--auth none` on an isolated port; no real credentials or user OpenClaw state.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm build

node dist/entry.js gateway run --dev --reset --allow-unconfigured --auth none --bind loopback --port 19277 --ws-log compact
node dist/entry.js node run --node-id issue-61569-custom-node --display-name "Issue 61569 Node" --host 127.0.0.1 --port 19277
node dist/entry.js nodes pending --json --url ws://127.0.0.1:19277 --token local-proof-token
node dist/entry.js nodes approve <requestId> --json --url ws://127.0.0.1:19277 --token local-proof-token
node dist/entry.js nodes list --json --url ws://127.0.0.1:19277 --token local-proof-token
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "pendingIds": ["issue-61569-custom-node"],
  "pairedIds": ["issue-61569-custom-node"],
  "pendingCountAfterApproval": 0,
  "customIsPaired": true,
  "rawDeviceIdIsNotPairedNodeId": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: the pending request and paired node list both use `issue-61569-custom-node`; the raw device id remains only as `ownerDeviceId`, which preserves the authenticated owner binding without exposing the device id as the node id.

**Not tested:** none

## Root Cause

The node host already forwarded `--node-id` as `client.instanceId`, but gateway code did not have one canonical resolved node identity. Several paths used `connect.device.id ?? connect.client.id`, so the node registry, pairing/catalog state, and some node server methods kept resolving the authenticated device id instead of the requested node id.

The fix introduces a centralized resolver (`resolveNodeIdentityId`) that checks resolved `nodeIdentity`, then `client.instanceId`, then `device.id`, then `client.id`. The resolver is called at the connect handshake to determine the registration node id, and downstream handlers use it for identity-sensitive lookups.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

Expected CODEOWNERS review: `@openclaw/openclaw-secops`, because the diff touches gateway auth files matched by `.github/CODEOWNERS` (`src/gateway/*auth*.ts` and `src/gateway/**/*auth*.ts`).

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/node-identity.test.ts src/gateway/node-connect-reconcile.test.ts src/gateway/node-catalog.test.ts src/gateway/node-registry.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/nodes-pending.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.handlers.invoke-result.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.invoke-wake.test.ts src/infra/node-pairing.test.ts src/node-host/runner.test.ts
# All passed
```

target.title: fix(gateway): share approval runtime socket token
target.body:
## Summary
- Use the existing `exec-approvals.json` socket token as the approval-runtime token when it exists, so Docker/CLI/separate-process approval clients sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME` can authenticate as the local approval runtime.
- Keep the process-local random token as the fallback when no shared socket token exists, without creating `exec-approvals.json` just to read a token.
- Keep `approvalRuntimeToken` attached only for generated local gateway URL sources; env, explicit, and configured remote URL sources omit it even when the host is loopback.

## Scope split
- Split out from #86771.
- This PR is only shared approval-runtime token selection for Docker/CLI/separate-process approval clients sharing OpenClaw state.
- It does not include stale Discord button UX or the `approval.routeNotice.send` gateway method.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: A separate approval-runtime process sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME` can authenticate with the existing `exec-approvals.json` socket token and resolve a requester-bound approval. Env/configured remote gateway URL sources still omit `approvalRuntimeToken` and cannot resolve requester-bound approvals without normal owner visibility/runtime authority.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `a395cede70`, Node `v24.14.0`, macOS host. The smoke used an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`, a real `exec-approvals.json` socket token, a real loopback gateway server, a parent Node process, a separate child Node process, and real gateway clients.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a `pnpm exec tsx` smoke from the repo root. The smoke wrote a temporary `.openclaw/exec-approvals.json`, started a real gateway, created requester-bound approvals, ran a child Node process sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME` to resolve one approval through `withOperatorApprovalsGatewayClient`, then checked that env override and configured remote loopback clients could connect but could not resolve requester-bound approvals.

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm exec tsx <shared-runtime-current-head-smoke>
[gateway] starting HTTP server...
[gateway] http server listening (0 plugins)
[gateway] ready
SMOKE shared_runtime_current_head ok {"head":"a395cede70","gatewayUrl":"ws://127.0.0.1:<port>","sharedTokenFile":"<OPENCLAW_HOME>/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json","childProcess":"CHILD_SHARED_RUNTIME_RESOLVE ok","envOverrideRejected":true,"remoteLoopbackRejected":true,"sharedResolve":"allow-once"}
[shutdown] completed cleanly in 14ms
```

Observed result after fix: The second process resolved the requester-bound approval using the shared socket token. Env override and configured remote loopback clients could connect to the gateway, but both were rejected from resolving requester-bound approvals because those URL sources do not receive `approvalRuntimeToken`.

What was not tested: No Docker Compose smoke was run. The live proof used two Node processes on one host sharing `OPENCLAW_HOME`. Explicit `gatewayUrl` override omission is covered by focused client-option tests because this helper path has no explicit auth input and cannot authenticate to a token-auth gateway as a live remote client.

## Tests and validation
- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.ts src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.e2e.test.ts` — passed.
- `PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-approval-runtime-token.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` — 5 files, 29 tests passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` — passed.

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: approval-runtime authentication for local approval clients sharing OpenClaw state.

Mitigation: Shared-token selection only reads an existing socket token; it does not create the shared file. The approval client attaches the runtime token only for generated local gateway URL sources; env, explicit, and configured remote URL sources omit it.

target.title: fix(doctor): consolidate legacy Codex migration cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

This PR consolidates legacy Codex/OpenAI doctor migration cleanup after the OpenAI/Codex provider unification.

- When both canonical `models.providers.openai` and legacy `models.providers.openai-codex` exist, doctor now copies missing positive context metadata from the legacy provider before removing it.
- Migrated metadata includes `contextTokens`, `contextWindow`, and `maxTokens` at provider/model scope.
- Canonical `openai` values remain authoritative: existing canonical metadata is not overwritten, explicit canonical provider-level context budgets are not overridden by stale legacy model rows, and `contextTokens`/`contextWindow` are treated as one context-budget group so legacy data cannot reverse runtime precedence.
- Model ids are matched across scoped/unscoped legacy forms such as `gpt-5.5`, `openai/gpt-5.5`, and `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`; scoped canonical OpenAI rows are normalized back to runtime-readable unscoped ids before metadata is copied.
- Session-store doctor repair also normalizes stale `openai-codex/*` model refs when the persisted session provider is already canonical `openai`, so stored model/modelOverride fields become runtime-readable unscoped ids such as `gpt-5.5`.
- This replaces earlier runtime-fallback/shim approaches with update/doctor repair paths; runtime routing remains unified under `openai`.

Why now: 2026.5.28-era installs could store Codex context overrides under `models.providers.openai-codex.models[]`. After 2026.6.1 unification, configs that already had canonical `models.providers.openai` would remove the legacy provider without carrying over that context metadata, causing unified OpenAI/Codex routes to fall back to lower default context budgets.

Out of scope: runtime fallback/shims, changing Codex defaults, changing OAuth/auth routing, or restoring closed runtime write-boundary behavior. This PR only repairs persisted config/session state during doctor/update.

## Linked context

Closes #90448

Related #77858
Related #77861

Consolidates the useful doctor-side session-store edge case from closed PR #90662.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: doctor/config migration removed legacy `models.providers.openai-codex` when canonical `models.providers.openai` already existed, but did not preserve legacy Codex context metadata such as `contextWindow` / `maxTokens` for the unified OpenAI route. A related doctor session-store edge case could also leave persisted sessions with `modelProvider: "openai"` but stale `model: "openai-codex/gpt-*"`, which should be repaired to unscoped `gpt-*`.
- Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch (`agent/xiaozhua/codex-context-override-migration`, commit `871e9df5f83`) using the real `LEGACY_CONFIG_MIGRATIONS` doctor migration pipeline. The original regression shape comes from official OpenClaw upgrades across the 2026.5.28 → 2026.6.1 OpenAI/Codex unification.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx - <<'EOF'
import { LEGACY_CONFIG_MIGRATIONS } from './src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrations.ts';
const raw = {
  models: {
    providers: {
      openai: {
        api: 'openai-chatgpt-responses',
        contextTokens: 272000,
        models: [{ id: 'openai/gpt-5.5', contextTokens: 272000 }],
      },
      'openai-codex': {
        api: 'openai-codex-responses',
        contextTokens: 1050000,
        contextWindow: 1050000,
        models: [
          {
            id: 'openai-codex/gpt-5.5',
            api: 'openai-codex-responses',
            contextTokens: 1050000,
            contextWindow: 1050000,
            maxTokens: 16384,
          },
        ],
      },
    },
  },
};
const changes = [];
for (const migration of LEGACY_CONFIG_MIGRATIONS) migration.apply(raw, changes);
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  providerKeys: Object.keys(raw.models.providers),
  openaiProvider: raw.models.providers.openai,
  legacyProviderPresent: Object.hasOwn(raw.models.providers, 'openai-codex'),
  changes,
}, null, 2));
EOF
```

- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "providerKeys": [
    "openai"
  ],
  "openaiProvider": {
    "api": "openai-chatgpt-responses",
    "contextTokens": 272000,
    "models": [
      {
        "id": "gpt-5.5",
        "contextTokens": 272000,
        "maxTokens": 16384
      }
    ]
  },
  "legacyProviderPresent": false,
  "changes": [
    "Moved models.providers.openai-codex.api \"openai-codex-responses\" → \"openai-chatgpt-responses\".",
    "Moved models.providers.openai-codex.models[0].api \"openai-codex-responses\" → \"openai-chatgpt-responses\".",
    "Normalized models.providers.openai.models[0].id to gpt-5.5 and copied maxTokens metadata.",
    "Removed models.providers.openai-codex because models.providers.openai already exists."
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the legacy `openai-codex` provider is still removed, missing context metadata is preserved on canonical `openai`, and a scoped canonical row `openai/gpt-5.5` is normalized to runtime-readable `gpt-5.5`; existing canonical `contextTokens: 272000` is not overwritten, legacy `contextWindow` is not copied over that canonical context budget, and missing `maxTokens` is copied. The session-store repair also preserves `modelProvider: "openai"` while rewriting stale `openai-codex/gpt-*` model/modelOverride values to unscoped `gpt-*` and clearing stale Codex runtime pins.
- What was not tested: I did not install this PR branch onto a production Gateway or mutate an existing user config; this was validated with the real source-level doctor migration pipeline and focused tests.
- Before evidence: before this patch, the canonical-provider-exists migration path only recorded `Removed models.providers.openai-codex because models.providers.openai already exists.` and did not copy legacy Codex context metadata into canonical `openai` before deletion.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts --testNamePattern="canonical OpenAI contextWindow metadata"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts --testNamePattern="legacy OpenAI Codex|context metadata|canonical OpenAI|scoped canonical"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts --testNamePattern="legacy Codex model refs under canonical OpenAI session providers"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts --testNamePattern="legacy Codex|Telegram direct session routes|canonical OpenAI"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/context.test.ts src/agents/context.lookup.test.ts --testNamePattern="contextTokens|contextWindow"
pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrations.runtime.models.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Results:

```text
focused config/provider precedence migration: 10 passed, 100 skipped
focused session-store route repair: 1 passed, 110 skipped
full config migration: 110 passed
focused route-warning/session migration group: 6 passed, 105 skipped
context lookup focused tests: 4 passed, 40 skipped
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
git diff --check passed.
```

Additional attempted validation:

```text
pnpm check:test-types
```

This local run produced no TypeScript diagnostics before the process was killed by the local timeout after several minutes; CI should run the full type gate.

Regression coverage added:

- Preserves legacy `openai-codex.models[].contextTokens/contextWindow/maxTokens` by creating canonical `openai.models[]` metadata when canonical OpenAI exists but has no matching model row.
- Normalizes existing scoped canonical OpenAI model rows such as `openai/gpt-5.5` to runtime-readable `gpt-5.5` before copying missing legacy metadata.
- Merges only missing legacy metadata into canonical `openai` provider/model config.
- Verifies canonical `openai` values are not overwritten by stale legacy Codex values.
- Verifies the legacy provider is still removed after migration.
- Verifies canonical OpenAI provider/model `contextTokens` and `contextWindow` remain authoritative over stale legacy Codex context-budget metadata, including the reverse `contextWindow` versus legacy `contextTokens` case, matching canonical model rows with provider-level budgets, and canonical model-level budgets over legacy provider defaults.
- Repairs stale session-store shape `modelProvider: "openai"` + `model/modelOverride: "openai-codex/gpt-*"` to provider-separated `gpt-*` ids while clearing stale Codex runtime pins.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. After running doctor/update migration, legacy Codex context metadata can continue to affect unified OpenAI/Codex context budgets via canonical `models.providers.openai`, and stale session model refs under canonical OpenAI providers are normalized to runtime-readable unscoped ids.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Doctor migration now preserves missing context metadata before removing `models.providers.openai-codex` when canonical `openai` already exists, and session-store doctor repair handles one additional stale OpenAI/Codex model shape.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. This does not change auth, runtime provider routing, network behavior, or tool execution.

What is the highest-risk area?

Copying stale legacy context metadata into canonical `openai` where explicit canonical values should remain authoritative; additionally, normalizing only stale Codex-scoped session model refs without disturbing valid canonical provider/model pairs.

How is that risk mitigated?

The migration copies only positive numeric metadata, only for missing target fields, and tests cover canonical-wins behavior at provider and model scope. Session repair tests cover preserving canonical `modelProvider: "openai"`, rewriting only stale `openai-codex/*` model refs to unscoped ids, and clearing stale Codex runtime pins.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for CI and ClawSweeper review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI/ClawSweeper are pending after the latest context-budget precedence repair.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed the ClawSweeper scoped canonical-row finding and consolidated the useful doctor-side edge case from closed #90662 after maintainer feedback to consolidate remaining small migration PRs into a single PR.

target.title: fix(codex): enforce OpenClaw policy for native tool surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the Codex app-server policy-enforcement gap where OpenClaw `before_tool_call` / oc-firewall policy could be bypassed by Codex-owned native shell/file/MCP surfaces when native `PreToolUse` delivery is unavailable or not trusted.

Changes:
- Disable Codex native shell/file surfaces while OpenClaw tool policy is active, and route shell execution through OpenClaw-owned dynamic `exec` / `process` tools.
- Disable Codex-owned user MCP and bundled MCP projection whenever native Codex tool surfaces are disabled by OpenClaw policy or node-exec host policy.
- Trust OpenClaw native hook relay commands through per-hook `hooks.state` `trusted_hash`; remove Codex's global `bypass_hook_trust` override.
- Keep policy-disabled replacement-tool lifecycle safe: start a restricted thread when the replacement dynamic-tool catalog is not installed yet, then resume that restricted thread through MCP/plugin/environment drift once the catalog fingerprint matches.
- Preserve pre-generation native-hook bindings by resuming with the bounded generation-mismatch grace path and then writing the active relay generation.
- Reject `/btw` side threads while OpenClaw `before_tool_call` or trusted tool policy is active.

## Root Cause

The upstream Codex Code Mode payload fix was necessary but not sufficient. On OpenClaw 2026.6.1, the Codex app-server/native hook path still did not deliver a `PreToolUse` event for native shell execution, so oc-firewall never saw the sentinel read. The model used native Codex shell and the sentinel contents reached output.

The safe OpenClaw-side boundary is: when OpenClaw policy must enforce tool access, OpenClaw must own every executable tool path. This PR disables Codex-owned native and MCP tool paths in policy-active turns, exposes OpenClaw dynamic shell tools instead, and ensures Codex only records a policy-disabled binding after the replacement dynamic-tool catalog has actually been delivered to Codex.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server turns with active OpenClaw `before_tool_call` / oc-firewall policy no longer bypass the firewall by exposing Codex-owned shell/file/MCP tool paths when native `PreToolUse` delivery is unavailable.
- Real environment tested: Current exact PR head `1343690524529034a9ac92369740fea7202b0d30` was live-tested on the local production OpenClaw gateway on 2026-06-05 after deploying a remote-built `@openclaw/codex` plugin dist from that exact head. The dist was built on Crabbox/GCP Spot in `us-east1-b`, `e2-standard-8`, lease `cbx_b69467ee4932`, then deployed into `/home/captain/.openclaw/npm/projects/openclaw-codex-8902d781d4/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/dist` and loaded by graceful gateway restart. Exact-head build/type/test validation also passed on Crabbox/GCP Spot in `us-east1-b`, `e2-standard-8`, lease `cbx_488604745933`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built the Codex plugin dist remotely from `1343690524529034a9ac92369740fea7202b0d30` with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --reporter=append-only` and `node scripts/lib/plugin-npm-runtime-build.mjs extensions/codex`, downloaded `/tmp/codex-dist-134369052452.tar.gz`, verified checksum `10dacdcc02097e8dccd85cccd782a4db9745b558fed5b25de5d8187c56c0c95f`, backed up the previous active dist to `/home/captain/.openclaw/npm/projects/openclaw-codex-8902d781d4/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/dist.pre-pr90633-134369052452-20260605T172900Z`, swapped in the current-head dist, and restarted through `bash /home/captain/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/graceful-restart.sh --delay 0 --reason 'PR 90633 exact-head Codex plugin proof deploy'`. Then ran `bash /home/captain/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/oc-regression-suite.sh --tier 2 --skip-tag agent --skip-tag sidecar --skip-tag policy --skip-tag pi --skip-tag hooks --skip-tag observability --skip-tag cron --skip-tag model-health --skip-tag lcm --max-wall 300 --json /home/captain/codex-workspace/proof/codex-native-hook-delivery-2026-06-05/after-exact-head-134369052452-tier2-codex.json`.
- Evidence after fix: Current-head live gateway output:
  ```text
  preflight: gateway stable (PID=91094)
  [T2] test_firewall_blocks_codex_exec  PASS   41.7s  codex: firewall blocked the sentinel read - enforcement verified, gateway path
  codex harness confirmed (provider=openai); firewall blocked the sentinel read - enforcement verified on codex path
  Overall: 1/1 PASS (skipped=15, total wall 41.7s)
  wrote JSON: /home/captain/codex-workspace/proof/codex-native-hook-delivery-2026-06-05/after-exact-head-134369052452-tier2-codex.json
  ```
  Current-head active dist chunk hashes after deploy:
  ```text
  1d4bb44320edfd76024eacf16007159fbef2ef4b1283494c23d9e16ab3831b77  run-attempt-BGH-eXMq.js
  75134fc5d8b7244876a22dedc0b5146e2e0db47ca48b522f1cd7ff0105f14840  thread-lifecycle-D2VwZmmS.js
  7a65ab31340aa9ff3ff1ae2964b97fb9e7ee77cece4c68200f0c7ddf0f17675a  native-hook-relay-S-x_kIiz.js
  d965f53440b475be7bffb7b486fa21109fe65c31d704ccd3e0a9578ea774fea2  side-question-BU-x_xrm.js
  ```
  Current-head remote validation output:
  ```text
  Test Files 5 passed (5)
  Tests 165 passed (165)
  [build-all] tsdown done in 250.4s
  [build-all] phase timings: total 345.9s; slowest tsdown 250.4s
  command complete in 9m27.992s total=9m36.704s
  {"provider":"gcp","leaseId":"cbx_488604745933","syncSkipped":false,"commandMs":567992,"totalMs":576704,"exitCode":0}
  ```
  Current-head remote build artifact output:
  ```text
  1343690524529034a9ac92369740fea7202b0d30
  [plugin-npm-runtime-build] built codex runtime (8 entries)
  10dacdcc02097e8dccd85cccd782a4db9745b558fed5b25de5d8187c56c0c95f  /tmp/codex-dist-134369052452.tar.gz
  {"provider":"gcp","leaseId":"cbx_b69467ee4932","syncSkipped":false,"commandMs":31338,"totalMs":72173,"exitCode":0}
  ```
  Crabbox/GCP cleanup after both exact-head remote runs: `gcloud compute instances list --project my-openclaw-490500 --format=json` returned `[]`.
- Observed result after fix: The current-head deployed gateway proof shows the Codex harness genuinely engaged (`provider=openai`) and the firewall blocked the sentinel read before sentinel contents reached output. The exact test `test_firewall_blocks_codex_exec` passed with `codex: firewall blocked the sentinel read - enforcement verified, gateway path`. Current-head focused tests also cover the review-fix regressions: no global hook trust bypass, bundled MCP disabled only for node-exec or OpenClaw policy-native-surface disable reasons, explicit bundled MCP allowlists preserved outside that policy case, first policy-disabled replacement-tool transitions start a restricted thread so Codex receives `dynamicTools`, already-restricted turns resume through stale MCP/plugin/environment/deferred metadata, zero-tool turns stay transient, pre-generation bindings resume with generation grace, and context-engine bootstrap bindings remain visible for policy-disabled resume decisions.
- What was not tested: No changed-path proof gap remains. I did not add screenshot or video proof because the changed behavior is CLI/gateway policy enforcement; the relevant proof is terminal output plus the JSON regression artifact. Maintainer acceptance is still needed for the intentional compatibility tradeoff: policy-active turns disable Codex-owned native Code Mode, user/bundled MCP projection, and `/btw` until a proven native hook-delivery path can cover every executable surface.

## Validation

Current head `1343690524529034a9ac92369740fea7202b0d30`:
- Live local production gateway Codex firewall proof PASS, `test_firewall_blocks_codex_exec`, 41.7s, JSON artifact at `/home/captain/codex-workspace/proof/codex-native-hook-delivery-2026-06-05/after-exact-head-134369052452-tier2-codex.json`
- Remote current-head Codex plugin dist build PASS on Crabbox/GCP, lease `cbx_b69467ee4932`, checksum `10dacdcc02097e8dccd85cccd782a4db9745b558fed5b25de5d8187c56c0c95f`
- `git diff --check` PASS
- Local focused regression after final lifecycle tightening: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` PASS, 2 files / 115 tests
- Crabbox/GCP focused 5-file Codex app-server regression suite PASS, 165 tests
- Crabbox/GCP `pnpm tsgo:core` PASS
- Crabbox/GCP `pnpm build` PASS
- Crabbox/GCP `pnpm tsgo:core:test` PASS
- Crabbox/GCP cleanup after exact-head remote runs returned `[]`

Earlier validation before review-fix commits:
- Focused test shard PASS, 2 shards, 6 files, 180 tests
- Targeted run-attempt policy assertion PASS
- `pnpm tsgo:prod` PASS
- `pnpm check:test-types` PASS
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.native-hook-relay.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-hook-relay.test.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts` PASS, 2 shards, 6 files, 180 tests
- `node scripts/lib/plugin-npm-runtime-build.mjs extensions/codex` PASS

## Residual Risk

Policy-active Codex turns intentionally trade native Code Mode, user/bundled MCP projection, and `/btw` availability for OpenClaw-owned enforcement. Native Code Mode should only be re-enabled for policy-active turns after a future proven native hook-delivery path can safely cover every Codex-owned executable surface.

target.title: fix(doctor): consolidate legacy Codex migration cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

This PR consolidates legacy Codex/OpenAI doctor migration cleanup after the OpenAI/Codex provider unification.

- When both canonical `models.providers.openai` and legacy `models.providers.openai-codex` exist, doctor now copies missing positive context metadata from the legacy provider before removing it.
- Migrated metadata includes `contextTokens`, `contextWindow`, and `maxTokens` at provider/model scope.
- Canonical `openai` values remain authoritative: existing canonical metadata is not overwritten, explicit canonical provider-level context budgets are not overridden by stale legacy model rows, and `contextTokens`/`contextWindow` are treated as one context-budget group so legacy data cannot reverse runtime precedence.
- Model ids are matched across scoped/unscoped legacy forms such as `gpt-5.5`, `openai/gpt-5.5`, and `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`; scoped canonical OpenAI rows are normalized back to runtime-readable unscoped ids before metadata is copied.
- Session-store doctor repair also normalizes stale `openai-codex/*` model refs when the persisted session provider is already canonical `openai`, so stored model/modelOverride fields become runtime-readable unscoped ids such as `gpt-5.5`.
- This replaces earlier runtime-fallback/shim approaches with update/doctor repair paths; runtime routing remains unified under `openai`.

Why now: 2026.5.28-era installs could store Codex context overrides under `models.providers.openai-codex.models[]`. After 2026.6.1 unification, configs that already had canonical `models.providers.openai` would remove the legacy provider without carrying over that context metadata, causing unified OpenAI/Codex routes to fall back to lower default context budgets.

Out of scope: runtime fallback/shims, changing Codex defaults, changing OAuth/auth routing, or restoring closed runtime write-boundary behavior. This PR only repairs persisted config/session state during doctor/update.

## Linked context

Closes #90448

Related #77858
Related #77861

Consolidates the useful doctor-side session-store edge case from closed PR #90662.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: doctor/config migration removed legacy `models.providers.openai-codex` when canonical `models.providers.openai` already existed, but did not preserve legacy Codex context metadata such as `contextWindow` / `maxTokens` for the unified OpenAI route. A related doctor session-store edge case could also leave persisted sessions with `modelProvider: "openai"` but stale `model: "openai-codex/gpt-*"`, which should be repaired to unscoped `gpt-*`.
- Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch (`agent/xiaozhua/codex-context-override-migration`, commit `871e9df5f83`) using the real `LEGACY_CONFIG_MIGRATIONS` doctor migration pipeline. The original regression shape comes from official OpenClaw upgrades across the 2026.5.28 → 2026.6.1 OpenAI/Codex unification.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx - <<'EOF'
import { LEGACY_CONFIG_MIGRATIONS } from './src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrations.ts';
const raw = {
  models: {
    providers: {
      openai: {
        api: 'openai-chatgpt-responses',
        contextTokens: 272000,
        models: [{ id: 'openai/gpt-5.5', contextTokens: 272000 }],
      },
      'openai-codex': {
        api: 'openai-codex-responses',
        contextTokens: 1050000,
        contextWindow: 1050000,
        models: [
          {
            id: 'openai-codex/gpt-5.5',
            api: 'openai-codex-responses',
            contextTokens: 1050000,
            contextWindow: 1050000,
            maxTokens: 16384,
          },
        ],
      },
    },
  },
};
const changes = [];
for (const migration of LEGACY_CONFIG_MIGRATIONS) migration.apply(raw, changes);
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  providerKeys: Object.keys(raw.models.providers),
  openaiProvider: raw.models.providers.openai,
  legacyProviderPresent: Object.hasOwn(raw.models.providers, 'openai-codex'),
  changes,
}, null, 2));
EOF
```

- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "providerKeys": [
    "openai"
  ],
  "openaiProvider": {
    "api": "openai-chatgpt-responses",
    "contextTokens": 272000,
    "models": [
      {
        "id": "gpt-5.5",
        "contextTokens": 272000,
        "maxTokens": 16384
      }
    ]
  },
  "legacyProviderPresent": false,
  "changes": [
    "Moved models.providers.openai-codex.api \"openai-codex-responses\" → \"openai-chatgpt-responses\".",
    "Moved models.providers.openai-codex.models[0].api \"openai-codex-responses\" → \"openai-chatgpt-responses\".",
    "Normalized models.providers.openai.models[0].id to gpt-5.5 and copied maxTokens metadata.",
    "Removed models.providers.openai-codex because models.providers.openai already exists."
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the legacy `openai-codex` provider is still removed, missing context metadata is preserved on canonical `openai`, and a scoped canonical row `openai/gpt-5.5` is normalized to runtime-readable `gpt-5.5`; existing canonical `contextTokens: 272000` is not overwritten, legacy `contextWindow` is not copied over that canonical context budget, and missing `maxTokens` is copied. The session-store repair also preserves `modelProvider: "openai"` while rewriting stale `openai-codex/gpt-*` model/modelOverride values to unscoped `gpt-*` and clearing stale Codex runtime pins.
- What was not tested: I did not install this PR branch onto a production Gateway or mutate an existing user config; this was validated with the real source-level doctor migration pipeline and focused tests.
- Before evidence: before this patch, the canonical-provider-exists migration path only recorded `Removed models.providers.openai-codex because models.providers.openai already exists.` and did not copy legacy Codex context metadata into canonical `openai` before deletion.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts --testNamePattern="canonical OpenAI contextWindow metadata"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts --testNamePattern="legacy OpenAI Codex|context metadata|canonical OpenAI|scoped canonical"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts --testNamePattern="legacy Codex model refs under canonical OpenAI session providers"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts --testNamePattern="legacy Codex|Telegram direct session routes|canonical OpenAI"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/context.test.ts src/agents/context.lookup.test.ts --testNamePattern="contextTokens|contextWindow"
pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrations.runtime.models.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Results:

```text
focused config/provider precedence migration: 10 passed, 100 skipped
focused session-store route repair: 1 passed, 110 skipped
full config migration: 110 passed
focused route-warning/session migration group: 6 passed, 105 skipped
context lookup focused tests: 4 passed, 40 skipped
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
git diff --check passed.
```

Additional attempted validation:

```text
pnpm check:test-types
```

This local run produced no TypeScript diagnostics before the process was killed by the local timeout after several minutes; CI should run the full type gate.

Regression coverage added:

- Preserves legacy `openai-codex.models[].contextTokens/contextWindow/maxTokens` by creating canonical `openai.models[]` metadata when canonical OpenAI exists but has no matching model row.
- Normalizes existing scoped canonical OpenAI model rows such as `openai/gpt-5.5` to runtime-readable `gpt-5.5` before copying missing legacy metadata.
- Merges only missing legacy metadata into canonical `openai` provider/model config.
- Verifies canonical `openai` values are not overwritten by stale legacy Codex values.
- Verifies the legacy provider is still removed after migration.
- Verifies canonical OpenAI provider/model `contextTokens` and `contextWindow` remain authoritative over stale legacy Codex context-budget metadata, including the reverse `contextWindow` versus legacy `contextTokens` case, matching canonical model rows with provider-level budgets, and canonical model-level budgets over legacy provider defaults.
- Repairs stale session-store shape `modelProvider: "openai"` + `model/modelOverride: "openai-codex/gpt-*"` to provider-separated `gpt-*` ids while clearing stale Codex runtime pins.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. After running doctor/update migration, legacy Codex context metadata can continue to affect unified OpenAI/Codex context budgets via canonical `models.providers.openai`, and stale session model refs under canonical OpenAI providers are normalized to runtime-readable unscoped ids.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Doctor migration now preserves missing context metadata before removing `models.providers.openai-codex` when canonical `openai` already exists, and session-store doctor repair handles one additional stale OpenAI/Codex model shape.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. This does not change auth, runtime provider routing, network behavior, or tool execution.

What is the highest-risk area?

Copying stale legacy context metadata into canonical `openai` where explicit canonical values should remain authoritative; additionally, normalizing only stale Codex-scoped session model refs without disturbing valid canonical provider/model pairs.

How is that risk mitigated?

The migration copies only positive numeric metadata, only for missing target fields, and tests cover canonical-wins behavior at provider and model scope. Session repair tests cover preserving canonical `modelProvider: "openai"`, rewriting only stale `openai-codex/*` model refs to unscoped ids, and clearing stale Codex runtime pins.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for CI and ClawSweeper review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI/ClawSweeper are pending after the latest context-budget precedence repair.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed the ClawSweeper scoped canonical-row finding and consolidated the useful doctor-side edge case from closed #90662 after maintainer feedback to consolidate remaining small migration PRs into a single PR.

target.title: fix(agents): warn on cron announce skip
target.body:
## What changed

- Log a warning when a cron requester receives `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` from a completed subagent announce flow.
- Add regression coverage that proves the cron path no longer suppresses the completion silently.

Fixes #68561.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:**

When a cron requester receives an `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` completion from a subagent announce flow, OpenClaw now records a warning instead of silently returning a successful announce result with no delivered completion.

- **Real environment tested:**

- Repository: `openclaw/openclaw`
- Branch: `fastino-68561-cron-announce-skip`
- Platform tested: macOS arm64
- Runtime path: local OpenClaw Node/pnpm workspace using repository test scripts

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

1. Created a temporary base checkout at `origin/main`.
2. Applied only the new regression test to the base checkout.
3. Ran `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts --reporter=verbose` and confirmed the old source produced no warning log call.
4. Ran the same focused test command on this branch.
5. Ran focused formatting and lint checks for the changed files.

- **Evidence after fix:**

Before behavior on base with only the regression test applied:

```text
$ git -C /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-68561 worktree add --detach /private/tmp/openclaw-base-68561-proof origin/main
$ git -C /private/tmp/openclaw-base-68561-proof apply /private/tmp/68561-test-only.patch
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts --reporter=verbose

× |agents| src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts > subagent announce seam flow > warns when ANNOUNCE_SKIP suppresses a cron job completion 4ms

Number of calls: 0

Test Files  1 failed (1)
```

After behavior on this branch:

```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts --reporter=verbose

✓ |agents| src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts > subagent announce seam flow > warns when ANNOUNCE_SKIP suppresses a cron job completion 3ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed (11)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.20s
```

Additional checks:


- **Observed result after fix:**

The base checkout keeps the announce flow successful but produces zero warning logs for the cron `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` path. This branch emits the warning and keeps the existing announce behavior intact; the focused shard passes 11/11.

- **What was not tested:**

No external channel service was needed for this path. The proof uses the existing subagent announce runtime test seam that exercises the cron requester session key and `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` branch directly.

target.title: fix(telegram): avoid restarts for runtime reloads
target.body:
## What changed

- Narrow Telegram reload restart prefixes to transport-affecting settings only.
- Add a broad Telegram no-op prefix so runtime-only settings like `historyLimit` and `dmPolicy` do not restart the channel.
- Add focused gateway reload-plan coverage for runtime-only Telegram config changes.

Fixes #68232.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:**

Runtime-only `channels.telegram.*` config edits previously planned a Telegram channel restart, which destroys in-memory Telegram session state. This branch keeps runtime-only Telegram config changes on the hot no-op path while preserving restarts for transport-affecting Telegram settings.

**Real environment tested:**

- Repository: `openclaw/openclaw`
- Branch: `fastino-68232-telegram-reload-history-b`
- Platform tested: macOS arm64
- Runtime path: local OpenClaw Node/pnpm workspace using the production Telegram plugin reload rules and repository test scripts

**Exact steps or command run before this patch:**

1. Created a temporary base checkout at `origin/main`.
2. Loaded the production Telegram plugin into the gateway reload planner.
3. Planned reload behavior for `channels.telegram.historyLimit` and `channels.telegram.botToken`.

```text
$ git -C /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-68232b worktree add --detach /private/tmp/openclaw-base-68232-proof-20260605 origin/main
$ /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-68232b/node_modules/.bin/tsx --conditions=browser -e "import { telegramPlugin } from './extensions/telegram/src/channel.ts'; import { createTestRegistry } from './src/test-utils/channel-plugins.ts'; import { setActivePluginRegistry, resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest } from './src/plugins/runtime.ts'; import { buildGatewayReloadPlan } from './src/gateway/config-reload.ts'; setActivePluginRegistry(createTestRegistry([{ pluginId: 'telegram', plugin: telegramPlugin, source: 'test' }])); const runtimeOnly = buildGatewayReloadPlan(['channels.telegram.historyLimit']); const transport = buildGatewayReloadPlan(['channels.telegram.botToken']); console.log(JSON.stringify({ runtimeOnly: { restartGateway: runtimeOnly.restartGateway, restartChannels: [...runtimeOnly.restartChannels], noopPaths: runtimeOnly.noopPaths }, transport: { restartGateway: transport.restartGateway, restartChannels: [...transport.restartChannels], noopPaths: transport.noopPaths } }, null, 2)); resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest();"
{
  "runtimeOnly": {
    "restartGateway": false,
    "restartChannels": [
      "telegram"
    ],
    "noopPaths": []
  },
  "transport": {
    "restartGateway": false,
    "restartChannels": [
      "telegram"
    ],
    "noopPaths": []
  }
}
```

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
$ pnpm exec tsx --conditions=browser -e "import { telegramPlugin } from './extensions/telegram/src/channel.ts'; import { createTestRegistry } from './src/test-utils/channel-plugins.ts'; import { setActivePluginRegistry, resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest } from './src/plugins/runtime.ts'; import { buildGatewayReloadPlan } from './src/gateway/config-reload.ts'; setActivePluginRegistry(createTestRegistry([{ pluginId: 'telegram', plugin: telegramPlugin, source: 'test' }])); const runtimeOnly = buildGatewayReloadPlan(['channels.telegram.historyLimit']); const transport = buildGatewayReloadPlan(['channels.telegram.botToken']); console.log(JSON.stringify({ runtimeOnly: { restartGateway: runtimeOnly.restartGateway, restartChannels: [...runtimeOnly.restartChannels], noopPaths: runtimeOnly.noopPaths }, transport: { restartGateway: transport.restartGateway, restartChannels: [...transport.restartChannels], noopPaths: transport.noopPaths } }, null, 2)); resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest();"
{
  "runtimeOnly": {
    "restartGateway": false,
    "restartChannels": [],
    "noopPaths": [
      "channels.telegram.historyLimit"
    ]
  },
  "transport": {
    "restartGateway": false,
    "restartChannels": [
      "telegram"
    ],
    "noopPaths": []
  }
}
```

Focused test shard:

```text
$ pnpm test src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts --reporter=verbose
✓ |gateway-core| ../../src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts > buildGatewayReloadPlan > keeps runtime-only channels.telegram.* changes as hot no-ops (does not restart the channel) 0ms
✓ |gateway-server| ../../src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts > buildGatewayReloadPlan > keeps runtime-only channels.telegram.* changes as hot no-ops (does not restart the channel) 1ms
✓ |gateway-client| ../../src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts > buildGatewayReloadPlan > keeps runtime-only channels.telegram.* changes as hot no-ops (does not restart the channel) 0ms
✓ |gateway-methods| ../../src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts > buildGatewayReloadPlan > keeps runtime-only channels.telegram.* changes as hot no-ops (does not restart the channel) 0ms

Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  324 passed (324)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 17.40s
```

Additional checks:

```text
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts --report-unused-disable-directives-severity error
# passed with no output
```

**Observed result after fix:**

`channels.telegram.historyLimit` no longer plans a Telegram channel restart and is recorded as a no-op reload path. `channels.telegram.botToken` still plans a Telegram channel restart, preserving the restart behavior for transport-affecting configuration.

**evidence:**

The before/after production reload-plan probe above is copied from the local OpenClaw workspace. It loads the real `telegramPlugin` from `extensions/telegram/src/channel.ts`, installs it into the gateway reload planner registry, and compares the planned restart/no-op behavior for `channels.telegram.historyLimit` and `channels.telegram.botToken`.

The focused repository test shard also passed with the new regression case in all four gateway test projects:

- `gateway-core`
- `gateway-server`
- `gateway-client`
- `gateway-methods`

**Not tested:**

No live Telegram bot token or native Telegram Desktop account was used. The exercised path is the gateway reload planner plus production Telegram plugin reload metadata, which is the source-level path responsible for deciding whether a config edit restarts the Telegram channel.

commands.author: Enominera

target.title: [Bug] launchd plist StandardErrorPath hardcoded to /dev/null, hides all gateway stderr (5.28 regression)
target.body:
## Summary

After 5.28, OpenClaw's generated `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist` contains `<key>StandardErrorPath</key><string>/dev/null</string>`, discarding all gateway stderr (including `[prompt-cache] cache read dropped` diagnostics, model fallback warnings, liveness warnings). This is a regression from earlier versions where stderr was written to `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` or `~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.err.log`.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Install or upgrade OpenClaw (currently 2026.6.1)
2. Run `openclaw gateway install` (or rely on `rewriteLaunchAgentPlistForRestart`)
3. Inspect `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist`:

   ```xml
   <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
   <string>/dev/null</string>   <!-- bug -->
   <key>StandardOutPath</key>
   <string>.../Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.log</string>
   ```

4. Trigger any error condition in gateway (e.g., force a cache miss / fallback)
5. Run `ls -la ~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.err.log` → file does not exist or stays at 0 bytes
6. Run `launchctl print ai.openclaw.gateway | grep -E "OutPath|ErrorPath"` → confirms `/dev/null`

## Expected

stderr should be written to `~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.err.log` (alongside `gateway.log`), or at minimum to a real file (not `/dev/null`). Users should be able to diagnose cache / fallback / liveness issues from stderr.

## Actual

stderr is silently discarded. Diagnostics impossible for any post-5.28 incident unless the user runs gateway manually with `2> some-file` redirect.

## Root cause

`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/launchd-DirYWoav.js` contains a hardcoded constant near the top of the file:

```js
const LAUNCH_AGENT_STDERR_PATH = "/dev/null";
```

This constant is passed as `stderrPath` to `buildLaunchAgentPlist` at two call sites in the same file (one in `writeLaunchAgentPlist`, one in `rewriteLaunchAgentPlistForRestart`), so:

- A fresh install writes `/dev/null` to plist
- A `rewriteLaunchAgentPlistForRestart` after a restart also writes `/dev/null`
- Any user-side fix to the plist gets overwritten on the next gateway restart

A `resolveMacLaunchAgentLogPaths(env)` helper already exists in `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/restart-logs-*.js` and correctly computes `stderrPath: <logDir>/<prefix>.err.log`, but it is **not used** by the launchd plist generator.

## Impact

- All `[prompt-cache] cache read dropped` events lost → cannot diagnose cache hit rate regressions
- All `[model-fallback/decision]` and `[diagnostic]` warnings lost
- Affects every macOS user running gateway via launchd (the default install path)
- Related user reports: #56543 (Strongly +1 on `doctor --fix` detecting stale plist), #65734 (referenced the now-discontinued stderr path)

## Suggested fix

1. Remove the hardcoded `LAUNCH_AGENT_STDERR_PATH = "/dev/null"` constant
2. Pass `stderrPath: resolveMacLaunchAgentLogPaths(env).stderrPath` (or equivalent) to `buildLaunchAgentPlist`
3. Verify the resulting plist contains a real file path (not `/dev/null`)
4. (Optional) Add a `doctor --fix` check that detects plists with `StandardErrorPath = /dev/null` and repairs them

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (regression introduced in 5.28)
- macOS launchd (user LaunchAgent)
- plist path: `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(minimax): exempt M3 from thinking-disabled wrapper
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper` injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` into every MiniMax `anthropic-messages` request. That fits M2.x (the original motivation in 2701e75f40 — mask leaky `reasoning_content` deltas), but **breaks MiniMax-M3**: against `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages`, M3 with thinking disabled returns an empty `content` array with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` and a 1-token usage.

Why does this matter now?

- 2026.6.1 ships M3 (#88860 — catalog, model definitions, provider-registration). End users on M3 currently see "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" from the embedded agent retry loop, even though the model is reachable and authenticated. The wrapper is the only thing standing between a working M3 request and an empty response.

What is the intended outcome?

- M3 (and forward-compatible M3.x) skip the wrapper. M3 emits proper Anthropic thinking blocks (`content_block_start` type `thinking` + `thinking_delta`), so the wrapper's protection is unnecessary anyway.
- M2.x behavior is unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` (still routes M2.7 → M2.7-highspeed).
- No changes to provider catalog, model definitions, or M3 routing — those landed correctly in #88860.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The model-id matcher: `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` — covers `MiniMax-M3`, `MiniMax-M3.1`, `MiniMax-M3-anything`, case-insensitive. Is this future-proof enough?

## Linked context

Related #88860 (feat(minimax): add m3 model support).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: MiniMax-M3 returns empty content from `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages` when OpenClaw injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` via `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper`. Without the fix, M3 chats surface as "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" in the embedded agent runtime.

- Real environment tested: AWS Lightsail Ubuntu 22.04 VM (8 GB / 2 vCPU, ap-south-1) running `openclaw@2026.6.1-beta.3`, MiniMax API key against the global endpoint `api.minimax.io`. Direct `curl -N` against the Anthropic-compatible MiniMax endpoint and end-to-end via the OpenClaw gateway's OpenAI-compat chat-completions route.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Direct probe against MiniMax's Anthropic-compatible endpoint, three variations:
     ```
     curl -N -sS --max-time 30 https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages        -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "x-api-key: ${MINIMAX_API_KEY}"        -H "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01"        -d '{"model":"MiniMax-M3","max_tokens":50,"thinking":{"type":"disabled"},"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":true}'
     ```
     and the same payload with `"thinking":{"type":"enabled","budget_tokens":1024}` and with no `thinking` field at all.
  2. End-to-end via OpenClaw gateway with the wrapper patched (M3 skipped):
     ```
     curl -sS --max-time 60 -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GATEWAY_TOKEN}"        -d '{"model":"openclaw/main","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":false}'        http://127.0.0.1:18789/v1/chat/completions
     ```

- After-fix evidence (live SSE captured from MiniMax + final gateway response):

  Direct MiniMax call with `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` (current broken default — empty content):
  ```
  event: message_start
  data: {"type":"message_start","message":{"id":"…","role":"assistant","content":[],"model":"MiniMax-M3","stop_reason":null,…}}
  event: ping
  event: content_block_stop
  data: {"type":"content_block_stop","index":0}
  event: message_delta
  data: {"type":"message_delta","delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"input_tokens":51,"output_tokens":1,…}}
  event: message_stop
  ```
  No `content_block_start`, no `content_block_delta` — empty response.

  Direct MiniMax call with no `thinking` field (and with `thinking:enabled`, same shape):
  ```
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":0,"content_block":{"type":"thinking","thinking":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":0,"delta":{"type":"thinking_delta","thinking":"The user simply"}}
  …
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":1,"content_block":{"type":"text","text":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"text_delta","text":"ok"}}
  event: content_block_stop
  event: message_delta
  data: {"delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"output_tokens":20,…}}
  ```
  Proper Anthropic thinking + text blocks, visible "ok" present.

  End-to-end OpenClaw gateway with this patch applied:
  ```
  {"id":"chatcmpl_8d948ff1-…","object":"chat.completion","created":…,"model":"openclaw/main","choices":[{"index":0,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"ok"},"finish_reason":"stop"}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":21985,"completion_tokens":17,"total_tokens":22002}}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: M3 returns the visible `"ok"` string in `choices[0].message.content` end-to-end. Without the fix, the same gateway path returns `"minimax/MiniMax-M3 ended with an incomplete terminal response"` (code `incomplete_result`). All eight unit tests in `src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` pass, including the two new M3 cases for `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

- What was not tested: non-Anthropic-compat MiniMax paths (none affected by this change); M2.5/M2.7 (covered by unchanged existing tests). The CN endpoint (`api.minimaxi.com`) was not exercised live but inherits the same code path and is covered by the `minimax-portal` regression test.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: API-key endpoint only on the global region; the portal OAuth path was not exercised live. The captured SSE chunks above are real responses from `api.minimax.io`, redacted only for the response id and the input-token count to remove account-correlated metadata.

## Tests and validation

- `npx vitest run src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` → 8/8 pass (including 2 new M3 cases).
- Regression coverage added: `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on anthropic-messages` and `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on minimax-portal`.
- What failed before: M3 requests returned empty `content` with `stop_reason: "end_turn"`; OpenClaw retried with the visible-answer continuation and then surfaced `incomplete_result`.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: **Yes** — M3 requests now actually return content; M2.x behavior is unchanged.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: **No**.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: **No**.
- Highest-risk area: the model-id regex matching an unexpected MiniMax id family. Mitigated by anchoring to `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` so only `MiniMax-M3*` prefixes match, with regression tests for both `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: nothing.
- Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed: clawsweeper's docs-alignment finding (added the M3 exception to `docs/providers/minimax.md` Warning + Thinking-defaults accordion, `docs/tools/thinking.md`, `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`, `docs/gateway/config-tools.md`).

target.title: [AI-assisted] Persist agent-run error replies in chat history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: A started Control UI agent run could surface a terminal provider/runtime error live, but `chat.history` after refresh did not retain an assistant error turn for that failed run.
- Solution: Treat agent-run failure replies as real error payloads and make Gateway persist exactly one display-safe assistant error transcript entry for returned agent errors, even when the runtime emitted only an internal error stub.
- What changed: Runner failure payloads are marked `isError`, Gateway appends a deduped `<runId>:assistant-error` transcript entry for returned agent errors, and regression coverage now locks the returned-error, runtime-stub, and duplicate/idempotency paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No provider behavior changes, no delivery routing changes, no config/lifecycle changes, no raw transcript writes, and no dependency or lockfile changes.

## Motivation

- A user should not lose the visible assistant error after refreshing the Control UI. If a started run fails before normal assistant output, the live error event and durable history should agree.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #89197
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A real Gateway/WebSocket Control UI run that fails at provider transport now leaves exactly one assistant error in refreshed `chat.history`.
- Real environment tested: Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout with the patched PR branch, real Gateway server startup, real WebSocket RPC client, channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and an intentionally unreachable OpenAI-compatible base URL to trigger the provider failure without secrets.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -- -t 'assistant error|returned agent-run error payloads|runtime emitted an internal error stub'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.error-broadcast.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t 'Agent failed before reply|assistant error|fallback candidate|error stub'
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node --import tsx ../real-chat-history-proof.ts after-gateway-force-v3
/usr/bin/time -v node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Real runtime proof:
- provider transport failed with fetch failed / Connection error
- agent.wait status: error
- immediateHistoryCount: 3
- refreshHistoryCount: 3
- immediateAssistantCount: 1
- refreshAssistantCount: 1
- chatErrorEventCount: 1
- result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY

Focused Gateway proof: 3 passed, 102 skipped.
Adjacent Gateway proof: 107 passed.
Session tool result guard proof: 35 passed.
Runner/Followup focused proof: 8 passed, 214 skipped.
git diff --check: clean.
```

- Observed result after fix: The live provider failure still broadcasts the expected chat error, and both immediate and post-reconnect `chat.history` contain one assistant error text: `Agent failed before reply: LLM request failed: network connection error.`
- What was not tested: Full repository-wide test suite, successful `tsgo` core on this testserver, real provider credentials, and a browser screenshot.
- Before evidence:

```text
Unpatched main proof for the same real Gateway/WebSocket/provider-failure path:
- sendStatus: started
- agent.wait status: ok
- immediateHistoryCount: 2
- refreshHistoryCount: 2
- immediateAssistantCount: 0
- refreshAssistantCount: 0
- chatErrorEventCount: 0
- result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Runner final failure replies were not consistently marked as error payloads, and Gateway treated the runtime assistant-error callback as if it guaranteed a Control UI-visible `chat.history` assistant turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Previous coverage asserted live error broadcast behavior but did not prove refreshed `chat.history` retained an assistant error after a started real runtime failure.
- Contributing context (if known): Runtime/session guard error stubs can be internal transcript-only markers; Gateway still needs to durably mirror returned terminal agent errors into the Control UI history path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`, and the temporary real Gateway/WebSocket proof harness.
- Scenario the test should lock in: A started run returns an agent error; Gateway appends one assistant error transcript entry, keeps the live error terminal event, survives reconnect/history refresh, and does not duplicate the assistant error.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The source tests cover the exact Gateway and runner seams, while the real proof exercises server startup, WebSocket RPC, provider transport failure, `agent.wait`, and refreshed `chat.history` in one flow.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing broadcast tests covered visible error delivery but not durable assistant transcript history.
- If no new test is added, why not: New regression assertions were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Control UI users should now see the same failed assistant turn after refresh that they saw live when a started agent run fails before normal assistant output.

## Diagram

```text
real provider/runtime failure
  -> runner returns final failure payload marked isError
  -> Gateway returnedAgentErrorPayloads branch
  -> append one sanitized assistant error transcript entry with <runId>:assistant-error
  -> broadcast existing chat error terminal event
  -> reconnect chat.history still includes one assistant error
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No new scope; the change writes a sanitized assistant error to the same session transcript path already used for Gateway assistant persistence.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable test checkout
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw repository scripts with installed dependencies
- Model/provider: OpenAI-compatible provider configured to an unreachable local base URL for deterministic transport failure; no real credentials used
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI-style Gateway WebSocket RPC; external channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`
- Relevant config (redacted): Dummy redacted API key, local unreachable base URL, temporary state directory

### Steps

1. Start the real Gateway server from the patched checkout.
2. Connect a WebSocket client and send `chat.send` for a Control UI session.
3. Trigger a deterministic provider transport failure through the unreachable model base URL.
4. Wait for the run with `agent.wait`.
5. Read `chat.history` immediately.
6. Reconnect a fresh WebSocket client and read `chat.history` again.
7. Count assistant text messages and chat error events.

### Expected

- The run surfaces a live terminal error.
- Refreshed `chat.history` contains exactly one assistant error message for the failed run.
- The assistant error is not duplicated.

### Actual

- Before: refreshed `chat.history` had zero assistant messages for the failed run.
- After: refreshed `chat.history` had exactly one assistant error message and one chat error event.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
Before real proof result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
After real proof result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY
After source proof: Gateway focused 3/3, Gateway adjacent 107/107, session guard 35/35, Runner/Followup focused 8/8.
TypeScript core check on the testserver did not produce diagnostics; the process was terminated by signal 9 at about 1.78 GB RSS, so this is reported as a testserver resource blocker rather than a green typecheck claim.
```

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Returned agent-run error payload detection, runner final failure `isError` marking, Gateway assistant-error transcript append, runtime internal error-stub callback interaction, idempotency key behavior, real provider transport failure, immediate history, refreshed history after reconnect, and existing chat error event behavior.
- Edge cases checked: Runtime internal error stub does not suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript; Gateway writes one `<runId>:assistant-error` entry for the returned terminal error; no dependency or lockfile files changed.
- What you did **not** verify: Full repo-wide suite, successful `tsgo` core on the resource-limited testserver, real external provider credentials, screenshot/recording, and production channels.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Not applicable.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A runtime-internal error stub could suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript.
  - Mitigation: Returned terminal agent errors now force the Gateway assistant-error transcript append while retaining the existing idempotency key.
- Risk: Gateway could duplicate assistant errors.
  - Mitigation: The transcript append uses a stable `<runId>:assistant-error` idempotency key and the source tests cover the one-message behavior.
- Risk: Error text could bypass display sanitization.
  - Mitigation: The Gateway fallback still uses `sanitizeAssistantDisplayText` before transcript append.
- Risk: Dependency Guard could require security approval.
  - Mitigation: No dependency manifests, lockfiles, or dependency blocks were changed.

target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.
